title: Readline Promises, Stream Injection, EOF, Abort, and Close Ownership · Type Recall
summary: A delayed maintainer review reconstructs readline promises, stream injection, eof, abort, and close ownership from revision, runtime, command, unknown input, validation result, request identity, response fixture, promise state, cache decision, output, abort, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for injected abortable readline session adapter after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Create one readline promises interface from injected input and output and distinguish interface ownership from stream ownership.
Pass an AbortSignal to pending questions, classify abort separately, and remove or one-shot listeners without leaks.
Handle EOF, close, error, quit, and repeated close idempotently while preserving an explicit final session outcome.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A review TypeScript team handles case 5654 during a narrow-terminal community science event. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove one readline interface, abortable questions, EOF handling, ownership, and idempotent close; no user-visible meaning may rely on color, cursor motion, or pointer input; then change one unknown JSON field, nested member, status, URL, or byte budget and re-run validation. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create one readline promises interface from injected input and output and distinguish interface ownership from stream ownership.
